llm.title: LLM Context — {0}
llm.autoGenerated: Auto-generated context to guide implementation by an AI assistant.
llm.instructionsText:
Implement the nodes in the order shown, respecting graph dependencies (nodes without dependencies first).

### Workflow
1. Read the full documentation for each node before implementing it
2. Implement the functionality following the project Design Guide
3. Verify that the node's incoming and outgoing connections are compatible
4. After completing each module, update its diary entry with:
   - Summary of what was implemented
   - Issues found and decisions made
   - Estimated implementation time

### Post-implementation Validation
- Ensure the data flow between connected nodes is correct
- Verify that interfaces (Figma/HTML) match the implementation
- Check that all input/output pins are properly wired

Each node is a modular, independent unit. Follow the implementation order to minimize cross-dependencies.
llm.topologicalNote: Nodes are ordered topologically by their dependencies (nodes without dependencies first).
screen.noChildren: Drag processes here or use context menu to assign them

guide.title: Usage Guide
guide.intro: Blueprint Docs is a 100% offline visual flowchart editor. Create node-and-edge graphs, document each element with Markdown, and export documentation to multiple formats.
guide.nodes.text:
Drag nodes from the left palette onto the canvas, or double-click the canvas to add a process.

You can also use the quick-action buttons below the palette. Nodes connect by dragging from an output pin (circle) to an input pin on another node.

Available node types: Start, Process, Decision, Document, SubGraph, Screen and End.
guide.connections.text:
To create a connection, click and drag from an output pin (right side of node) to an input pin (left side) of another node.

The connection type is assigned automatically based on node types:
- Screen → Screen: Navigation (cyan dashed line)
- Screen → Process: User action (red dotted line)
- Process → Screen: Data → UI (purple dotted line)
- Process → Process: Data flow (solid gray line)
guide.screens.text:
Screen nodes can contain child nodes (processes, decisions, documents). Drag a node onto a Screen to assign it as a child task, or use the context menu "Link to Screen".

Child tasks are displayed inside the Screen container and move with it.

You can also add tasks from the properties panel using the "+ Add Task" button in the "Associated Tasks" section.

To resize a Screen, select it and drag the white triangle at the bottom-right corner, or adjust the Width and Height values in the properties panel.
guide.documentation.text:
Select a node or connection to see its properties panel on the right. There you can:
- Edit the name and short description
- Write Markdown documentation with live preview
- Use the "Template" button to insert predefined docs
- Set implementation order and status (Pending/In Progress/Done)
- Add entries to the implementation diary
- For Screens: add Figma and HTML prototype URLs, adjust dimensions, and manage associated tasks
guide.saving.text:
Use the "Save" or "Save As..." buttons in the toolbar.

**Conditions**:
- If your browser supports the File System Access API (Chromium/Edge): saves a folder with project.json + individual docs/
- If the API is not available: downloads a .json file as fallback
- To open a saved project, use "Open" and select the folder containing project.json
- Downloaded JSON projects are loaded manually from the file system
guide.exporting.text:
Two export modes are available:

**Export Docs (Markdown)**:
Generates a docs/ folder with:
- An index.md with node and connection tables
- One .md file per node with its documentation
- design-guide.md with the design guide
- An LLM context file (CLAUDE.md, AGENT.md, etc.)

**Export HTML**:
Generates a complete static site with a navigation index.html + individual docs/.

**LLM target selector**:
Choose the context file format for your preferred AI assistant.
guide.templates.text:
Use the template selector in the toolbar to load predefined graphs:
- **Simple Process**: basic linear flow
- **Decision Tree**: branching structure
- **AI Pipeline**: multi-stage data flow
- **Scientific Calculator**: full example with screen hierarchy

Applying a template discards current changes (confirmation is shown).
guide.filters.text:
Filters let you focus on specific aspects of the graph:

- **All**: shows all nodes and connections
- **Data Flow**: hides screens and navigation connections
- **Interfaces**: shows only screens, their child tasks, and interface connections

Filters do not modify data, only the display.
guide.shortcuts.text:
- **Delete / Backspace**: remove the selected node or connection
- **Escape**: deselect everything and close the context menu
- **Double-click canvas**: add a process at that position
- **Mouse wheel**: zoom in/out
- **Drag on canvas**: pan
- **Drag from output pin**: create a connection
guide.offline.text:
Blueprint Docs runs 100% in the browser, with no server or Internet connection required. All data is stored locally.

You only need a local HTTP server (such as the included serve.ps1) because ES modules use import/fetch. Once loaded, the application is fully self-contained.
